caption: The video presents a slide titled "Test intuitive economic interventions: Africa," which displays a bar graph illustrating the additional school years gained per $100 spent on various interventions. The interventions listed include unconditional cash transfers, conditional cash transfers, merit scholarships, free primary school uniforms, deworming, and providing information on educational returns to parents. The graph shows that providing information on educational returns to parents yields the highest number of additional school years (20.7), followed by deworming (13.9). The other interventions have significantly lower impacts, with unconditional cash transfers showing the least effect (0.02 additional school years). This visual data suggests that certain interventions can be much more effective than others in increasing school attendance and education duration in Africa.
